summary: Clean, slightly fast‑tuned recording captures a peaceful set highlighted by a jammy Cassidy and strong Stagger Lee guitar work.
review: The tape sounds clean but runs a bit fast, giving Jerry’s vocals an unusually high pitch and adding a faint layer of crowd noise. Musically the show is praised for its quiet, laid‑back vibe with standout jams on “Cassidy” and impressive guitar work during “Stagger Lee.”

ai_rating.rationale: Positive comments about the performance and notable song moments outweigh minor recording speed concerns; enough detailed reviews to form a solid but not unanimous view.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clean overall with some crowd noise; reviewers note the tape may be playing slightly fast, making vocals sound higher than normal.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Described as a quiet, peaceful corner of the Deadhead universe rather than a big‑ticket show; flow is relaxed and intimate.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Voice sounds higher than usual, possibly due to tape speed; guitar work on Cassidy receives praise.
